resources.string: Scan for detectable nearby devices, then follow the changing signal strength to help locate one.
Nearby device access is required to scan.
Location permission is required by older Android versions for Bluetooth scanning.
Bluetooth must be turned on to scan.
Bluetooth Low Energy is not available on this device.
Bluetooth scanning could not start. Try again in a moment.
Location services must be turned on for Bluetooth scanning on this Android version.
Move slowly and watch for a stronger reading. Signal strength can fluctuate because of walls, bodies, interference, and device orientation.
Waiting for this device to advertise again…
Start scanning to update the signal.
All functions happen locally on your device, with no internet connection required. This app is entirely open source, and the code is available for inspection on GitHub.

Bluetooth Device Finder scans for nearby Bluetooth Low Energy devices only while the app is open. Android may require Nearby devices or location permission, depending on the Android version. Device names, addresses, and signal readings are processed temporarily on your device and are not saved as scan history or transmitted. Android may temporarily preserve visible screen state when recreating the app. The app has no internet permission and does not use accounts, analytics, advertising, tracking, or remote crash reporting. It does not derive your physical location.

Bluetooth Device Finder helps locate detectable Bluetooth devices using changing signal strength. It does not estimate exact distance, identify hidden cameras, or guarantee that every Bluetooth device can be found. Devices must be powered on, within range, and actively detectable.
